# Vendor

Third-party runtimes packaged **into this tree** so a lane never depends on what
happens to be installed on the host. Everything here is a build artifact produced
by a `scripts/` bootstrapper — it is not source code and (like `data/`) is not
meant to be committed; regenerate it instead.

## `vendor/libreoffice/`

A self-contained, **relocatable** LibreOffice used by the office lane to
pre-convert LibreOffice/legacy formats (odt/ods/odp, doc/xls/ppt, rtf) to their
OOXML sibling, and by the figure lane to render emf/wmf images. Once present it is
discovered automatically by `office_convert.find_soffice()` — no `PATH`, no
`DOC2MD_LIBREOFFICE` env var needed.

Materialize / rebuild it with:

```
python3 scripts/setup_libreoffice.py --rpms /path/to/libreoffice/rpms   # first time
python3 scripts/setup_libreoffice.py                                    # no-op if installed
python3 scripts/setup_libreoffice.py --force                            # rebuild from carried rpms
python3 scripts/setup_libreoffice.py --uninstall                        # remove it
```

Layout (all paths inside the launcher derive from its own location, so the tree
can move without edits):

| path | what | commit? |
|---|---|---|
| `bin/soffice` | relocatable launcher (generated) | no |
| `root/` | extracted RPM filesystem (~617 MB, regenerable) | no |
| `rpms/` | carried `.rpm` source (~206 MB) — lets `--force` rebuild offline | optional |
| `lohome/` | LibreOffice user profile / `HOME` (regenerated on first run) | no |

The extracted RPMs are architecture/glibc-specific (x86-64 here); rebuild from
matching rpms on a different platform.
